audits.excerpt: Grief came through dry and gray. Ambition burned thin and sharp. Devotion lingered longest.
audits.reason: Jaina's read of the blade turns into abstract emotional taxonomy instead of staying inside bodily catches passing through a real guard body and the stone. The moment loses sensory friction exactly where the scene promises it.
audits.suggestedFix: Above her, the guard's step dragged for half a pace, then squared again. The next pulse left old ash on her tongue and a hard, upright strain that still wanted orders. Devotion lasted.

audits.excerpt: Lor'themar was somewhere above them with road dust still on his boots, trying to force sense into a court that had condemned the only two people in Capital City who understood the weapon in the vault.
audits.reason: The paragraph reports political context from above the cells in dossier mode. It needs one corridor sound or material interruption so the information stays prison-bound rather than floating as recap.
audits.suggestedFix: A cart wheel thudded somewhere in the service dark above. Voices rose, blurred, and died in the stone. Lor'themar was up there somewhere with road dust still on him, trying to force sense into rooms that moved paper faster than doors.

audits.excerpt: He had written it all down in that careful hand of his, margins neat, argument exact, horror tucked inside scholarship so cleanly it almost passed for virtue.
audits.reason: This memory slides into polished conceptual summary instead of cashing out through a prison-sourced trigger. The line is smart, but still too essay-shaped for a cell scene under active blade pressure.
audits.suggestedFix: She could still see the chalk under her own nails from the sanctum. Kel'Thuzad's hand had been careful on the page, margins neat enough to make the horror look orderly.

audits.excerpt: "I left Dalaran because no one believed the readings. I crossed your border alone because I thought proof would force the world to behave."
audits.reason: The confession starts reading like arc recap rather than cell speech. Jaina needs one hard contact beat inside the sentence so it sounds spoken through irons and stone, not narrated from outside the scene.
audits.suggestedFix: Her wrist ring grated when she shifted. "I left Dalaran because no one believed the readings. I crossed your border alone because I thought proof would force the world to behave."

audits.excerpt: "Millhaven," she said. "Andorhal. Scholomance. Northrend. I kept telling myself I was the one who knew what came next."
audits.reason: The named-place run functions like chapter-summary shorthand. Without a local carrier, the wall-talk briefly leaves the room.
audits.suggestedFix: She touched the masonry between them with each name. "Millhaven. Andorhal. Scholomance. Northrend. Every time I thought seeing it first meant I owned what came after."

audits.excerpt: They had conviction and no friction. No one at their shoulder who could say no, that's the wrong piece, start again.
audits.reason: The diagnosis is correct but phrased as thematic abstraction. It wants a shorter, harder prison-voice version with less essay language.
audits.suggestedFix: Her wrist ring clicked once against stone. "They had no one at the shoulder to catch the bad turn early. No one close enough to say no and make it stick."

audits.excerpt: The way it sampled each mind that crossed the vault corridor. The flavors it held onto. The way devotion lasted longest. The way grief burned out faster.
audits.reason: The blade report becomes conceptual sorting language when this section should stay usable and bodily. The scene needs the information in field terms Sylvanas can act on.
audits.suggestedFix: She tapped the next interval against the wall. "It skims a mind and leaves something behind. Grief burns off fast. Devotion hangs. That's the one it keeps worrying."

audits.excerpt: The prison had taken sight, touch, movement, any honest route between them. They could still pass the blade through masonry.
audits.reason: The scene exits on a thesis sentence instead of one last prison object or answering sound. That makes the close feel summarized rather than lodged in the room.
audits.suggestedFix: Jaina kept her palm on the mortar seam. On the far side, Sylvanas's chain answered once. Between those two small sounds, the blade still had somewhere to go.

audits.excerpt: The proportions were wrong in ways no armor could explain. They belonged to something that treated doors and walls as rules for smaller creatures.
audits.reason: Sylvanas's POV drifts from tactical read into grand authorial flourish. The scale is real, but it needs to stay measurable against ceiling, bars, and wings.
audits.suggestedFix: Its wing joints had tucked themselves tight just to fit; even so, the tips nearly brushed bar and wall. Nothing that big should have entered a room this small without splintering it.

audits.excerpt: It studied her face with scholarly patience, as if this were another chamber briefing and she had merely arrived late.
audits.reason: This uses one of the banned comparison crutches and replaces threat with a neat conceptual simile. The passage needs pressure, not a tidy briefing analogy.
audits.suggestedFix: It watched without blinking, patient enough to make the iron on her ankle feel lighter and more useless by the breath.

audits.excerpt: "How much plague could spread, how much fear it could buy, how many people death would force into line before he broke."
audits.reason: The villain's explanation lifts into systems-talk about yield and fear instead of cashing out in concrete plague infrastructure. That weakens the room's physicality and the menace.
audits.suggestedFix: "How many ovens took the flour. How many cellars stacked it. How many frightened men took orders faster once the dead were in the streets." The voice stayed calm.

audits.excerpt: "The blade already knows more than it did this morning. Your prince taught it. It knows what devotion tastes like now. It will use that."
audits.reason: This is important information, but it arrives as abstract operating-summary rather than as a line pinned to the vault pulse and the creature's immediate reaction to it.
audits.suggestedFix: Frostmourne thudded overhead. The creature tipped its head. "It knows what devotion tastes like now," it said. "Your prince taught it that much."

audits.excerpt: The grain road. Andorhal. Barov country. Stratholme. Useful strangers. Tidy help arriving where it could do the most work and cost the least suspicion.
audits.reason: Sylvanas's recognition starts in strong tactical suspicion, then slips into dossier shorthand. She needs one concrete remembered carrier per face before the pattern claim lands.
audits.suggestedFix: The empty wagon on the Millhaven road. The trader east of Andorhal. The half-seen shape by the Barov trees. The aide at Lor'themar's tent. The Stratholme merchant with the ledger under his arm.

audits.excerpt: Musical in a way Common could not carry.
audits.reason: The language beat lands as abstract philology before it lands as bodily impossibility. That order should reverse.
audits.suggestedFix: "Kalar'shorel'anor." The syllables came clean and old. Something in the back of her jaw locked. Not Common. Not any living form she had heard spoken in camp or court.

audits.excerpt: Fed them a name here, a road there, a warning when it served. Cleared the next stretch of road before they reached it. Let them see exactly enough to keep them moving where it wanted them moving.
audits.reason: The memory cascade explains the conspiracy in planner voice instead of letting remembered textures do the work. This is the biggest remaining over-summary in the chapter.
audits.suggestedFix: In the returned torchlight the five farewells hit one after another: wagon creak at Millhaven, mud off the Andorhal road, evening air by the Barov trees, canvas snapping at Lor'themar's tent, market soot in Stratholme. Same blessing each time. Same hand underneath.

audits.excerpt: Farstriders could listen for the phrase. Quartermasters could note it in road ledgers and camp rolls. Archive mages could compare it against old tongues and dead records once the bars opened a crack.
audits.reason: The sequel hook turns into explicit action-plan prose while Sylvanas is still chained in the cell. The scene needs a rougher, more immediate transfer shape, not an operations memo.
audits.suggestedFix: Chain clicked against the cot as she sat forward. One phrase for Farstrider ears. One question for quartermasters. One scrap for any mage old enough to know what language had just walked through the wall.
